researcher_notes: Florida's UCC system is unique among US states in being fully PRIVATIZED since October 1, 2001. The filing office operations are run by Image API, LLC (now Docufree) / FloridaUCC, LLC under contract with the Florida Department of State per F.S. 679.527. Despite privatization, the system is statutorily required to be publicly accessible via the internet. There is NO authorized searcher program -- searching is free and open to all. Florida's non-standard search logic means ALL debtor name errors are fatal per Florida Supreme Court precedent. The fee schedule page at floridaucc.com requires JavaScript and could not be scraped; however, statutory fees were confirmed from F.S. 679.525 (2025 Florida Statutes).

filing_office.statutory_authority: Florida Department of State, Division of Corporations
filing_office.statutory_reference: Florida Statutes Chapter 679 (Uniform Commercial Code: Secured Transactions), specifically F.S. 679.527 (Florida Secured Transaction Registry)
filing_office.operational_note: While the Division of Corporations is the statutory filing office, ALL operational functions have been privatized since October 1, 2001 to Image API, LLC (now Docufree). FloridaUCC, LLC, in partnership with the Florida Bankers Association, operates the Florida Secured Transaction Registry (FSTR) under contract with the Department of State.

private_operator.entity_name: Image API, LLC (now Docufree) / FloridaUCC, LLC
private_operator.responsibilities: All filings, forms, photocopy and certification requests, and database availability

state_retained_rights: Sole and exclusive ownership of all materials and records in the registry
Right to immediately reclaim control if contractor fails to perform or faces insolvency
Authority to develop performance standards ensuring accuracy, completeness, and good user service
filing_office.fixture_filings_note: Fixture filings and filings related to timber, minerals, and crops are filed with the Clerk of the Circuit Court in the relevant county, NOT with the state filing office

statutory_authority.agency_name: Florida Department of State, Division of Corporations
statutory_authority.note: The Division of Corporations (Sunbiz) UCC information page redirects all UCC inquiries to the private operator (FloridaUCC, LLC)

private_operator.entity_name: FloridaUCC, LLC / Image API, LLC (now Docufree)
private_operator.mailing_address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, P.O. Box 5588, Tallahassee, FL 32314
private_operator.physical_address: 2002 Old St. Augustine Rd., Bldg. D, Tallahassee, FL 32301
private_operator.office_hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EST, weekdays, excluding state holidays

authorization_model.model_type: Open Public Access (No Authorized Searcher Program)
authorization_model.description: Florida has NO formal 'authorized searcher' program. The Florida Secured Transaction Registry (FSTR) is statutorily required to be 'open to the public and accessible through the Internet' under F.S. 679.527. Searching the UCC database is completely free and does not require an account, login, or registration of any kind. Filing UCC documents online requires creating a free account on the FSTR portal. The system is self-service only -- as of April 17, 2005, FloridaUCC, LLC staff no longer perform searches on behalf of users.

comparison_to_other_states.vs_delaware: Unlike Delaware, which has a mandatory Authorized Searcher program with no public portal, Florida is fully open to the public
comparison_to_other_states.vs_georgia: Unlike Georgia, which requires at minimum a free Limited-Use account to search, Florida requires no account at all for searching
comparison_to_other_states.vs_alabama: Similar to Alabama in having open access, but Florida's system is privatized while Alabama's is operated directly by the Secretary of State

for_searching.application_forms: None required -- searching is free and open to the public without any account
for_searching.fees: FREE for online index/image searches via the FSTR portal
for_searching.entity_eligibility: Open to the public -- any individual or entity
for_searching.technology_requirements: Web browser with JavaScript enabled (the FSTR portal at floridaucc.com is a JavaScript-rendered single-page application)
for_searching.training: None required. Help documentation available at https://floridaucc.com/help

for_online_filing.fees: No registration fee. Standard filing fees apply per transaction.
for_online_filing.entity_eligibility: Open to the public -- any individual or entity
for_online_filing.approval_process: Self-service; instant; no approval step required

for_ach_debit_account.description: Frequent filers may establish an ACH Debit Account with FloridaUCC, LLC for streamlined payment processing
required_documents: Completed 'Authorization Agreement for Automatic Debits' form
Voided check from the designated bank account
for_ach_debit_account.approval_process: Submit form and voided check to FloridaUCC, LLC
for_ach_debit_account.form_url: https://www.floridaucc.com/uccweb/ach.aspx (JavaScript-rendered; may need to request form directly)

for_xml_api_access.description: Programmatic filing access via UCC XML API for bulk/automated electronic filing
for_xml_api_access.documentation: UCC XML API Implementation Guide (PDF published by Florida Department of State)

technical_details.submission_format: XML filing document submitted as string parameter

for_xml_api_access.enrollment_process: Not fully documented publicly -- contact FloridaUCC, LLC at help@FLORIDAUCC.com or 850-222-8526 for details

requirements_for_authorization.confidence: High for search/filing basics; Medium for ACH and API details

online_search.database_coverage: UCC financing statement images and index data from January 1997 to present
online_search.search_logic: NON-STANDARD -- Florida does NOT use standard UCC search logic per IACA guidelines. The system displays the entire alphabetical database index and positions results at the point where the searched name would appear. Results show 20 entries per page and the user navigates forward/backward through the index. A 'browse' feature allows users to explore the database when unsure of exact debtor name. CRITICAL: All debtor name errors are fatal per the Florida Supreme Court (1944 Beach Blvd., LLC v. Live Oak Banking Co.).
online_search.search_logic_legal_impact: Because Florida's filing office has no 'standard search logic,' the UCC section 9-506(c) safe harbor from debtor name errors does NOT apply. An empirical study found that under the Florida Supreme Court's zero-tolerance test, 32% of UCC filings against registered organizations (over 230,000 filings) are potentially ineffective by reason of name errors alone.
online_search.technology_note: The FSTR portal is a JavaScript-rendered single-page application. It will NOT work with JavaScript disabled or with basic web scrapers.
online_search.staff_search: NOT AVAILABLE -- discontinued April 17, 2005. All searches are self-service only.

xml_api_filing.description: UCC XML API for programmatic/bulk electronic filing via HTTP POST
xml_api_filing.confidence: Medium -- API exists but full enrollment process not publicly documented

mail.address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, P.O. Box 5588, Tallahassee, FL 32314

courier_in_person.address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, 2002 Old St. Augustine Rd., Bldg. D, Tallahassee, FL 32301
courier_in_person.hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EST, weekdays, excluding state holidays

fax.available: Unknown -- no fax number found during research

fee_schedule.important_note: The fees listed below are from the statutory text of F.S. 679.525 (2025 Florida Statutes). The official FSTR fee page at https://floridaucc.com/fees requires JavaScript and could not be independently confirmed via web scraping. The vendor (FloridaUCC, LLC) may charge additional service fees beyond statutory amounts. All fees are NONREFUNDABLE.

ucc_1_initial_financing_statement.base_fee: $25.00 (first page -- includes cost of filing a termination statement)
ucc_1_initial_financing_statement.general_revenue_surcharge: $10.00 (additional fee on first page of UCC-1, deposited to state General Revenue Fund, not included in contractor compensation calculation)

ucc_3_continuation.fee: Same as amendment fee structure ($12.00 base)
ucc_3_continuation.timing: Must be filed within 6 months before the 5-year expiration of the original financing statement

ucc_3_termination.fee: Included in original UCC-1 filing fee (no additional charge)
ucc_3_termination.statutory_reference: F.S. 679.525(1)(a) -- UCC-1 fee 'shall include the cost of filing a termination statement'

fixture_filings.note: Filed with Clerk of Circuit Court, NOT state filing office. Fees per Chapter 28 (not F.S. 679.525)

ucc_11_certified_information_request.fee: Approximately $15-20 per request (exact amount needs verification from floridaucc.com/fees)
ucc_11_certified_information_request.description: Formal certified search via UCC-11 Information Request form
ucc_11_certified_information_request.note: The older statute F.S. 15.091 set the search fee at $20 per debtor name, but this statute predates privatization. Current vendor fee may differ.
ucc_11_certified_information_request.confidence: Medium -- exact current amount needs verification

expedited_processing.fee: $10.00 surcharge for same-day processing (reported by community sources; not confirmed in statute)

fee_schedule.refund_policy: All UCC fees are nonrefundable processing fees. No refunds issued by FloridaUCC, LLC for UCC documents that cannot be filed.

third_party_service_company_fees_for_ref.source: Florida Filing & Search Services, Inc. (third-party, NOT official state fees)
third_party_service_company_fees_for_ref.ucc_search_only: $30.00 (company service fee, does not include state fees)
third_party_service_company_fees_for_ref.ucc_filing_via_fstr: $30.00 (company service fee, does not include state fees)
third_party_service_company_fees_for_ref.note: These are third-party service company fees charged ON TOP OF state filing fees. Provided for reference only.

fee_schedule.confidence: High for statutory fees; Medium for vendor-specific operational fees

non_standard_search_logic.description: Florida does NOT implement 'standard search logic' as defined by the IACA or UCC Revised Article 9. The FSTR displays the entire alphabetical index rather than filtering results through a defined search algorithm. This means the UCC section 9-506(c) safe harbor from debtor name errors does NOT apply in Florida.
non_standard_search_logic.case_law: 1944 Beach Blvd., LLC v. Live Oak Banking Co. (Florida Supreme Court) -- held that FSTR had no standard search logic, so all debtor name errors are 'seriously misleading' and render the financing statement ineffective
non_standard_search_logic.practical_impact: An empirical study by Prof. Lynn M. LoPucki found that 32% of Florida UCC filings against registered organizations (over 230,000 filings) may be ineffective due to name errors alone
non_standard_search_logic.searcher_implication: Searchers must use the EXACT legal name of the debtor. No nickname, abbreviation, or variation will be returned. The 'browse' feature can help, but requires manual review of adjacent entries.

privatization_concerns.description: Florida's UCC filing system has been the subject of academic debate. The privatization arrangement has been criticized for the lack of standard search logic and the resulting high error rate. However, the state retains full ownership and oversight authority.

financing_statement_duration.continuation_window: Continuation statement must be filed within 6 months before expiration to maintain the perfected security interest
financing_statement_duration.lapse_consequence: If no continuation is filed, the financing statement lapses and the security interest becomes unperfected

key_legal_considerations.recording_consent: Florida is a TWO-PARTY consent state for call recording (F.S. 934.03) -- relevant for any phone outreach to the filing office

items_requiring_outreach.detail: The official fees page at https://floridaucc.com/fees requires JavaScript to render and could not be scraped during research. Statutory fees from F.S. 679.525 are confirmed, but the vendor (FloridaUCC, LLC) may charge different or additional operational fees. Secondary community sources report conflicting amounts ($20 vs $25 for electronic UCC-1 filings, which may not reflect the $10 statutory surcharge).
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to request the complete current fee schedule. Alternatively, visit floridaucc.com/fees in a JavaScript-enabled browser.

items_requiring_outreach.item: UCC-11 certified search exact fee and turnaround time
items_requiring_outreach.detail: The exact fee for a UCC-11 Information Request (certified search) could not be confirmed. The older statute F.S. 15.091 set the search fee at $20, but this predates privatization. Current vendor fee may differ.
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 to confirm the UCC-11 certified search fee, turnaround time, and submission process (online vs. mail only).

items_requiring_outreach.item: UCC XML API enrollment process and eligibility
items_requiring_outreach.detail: A UCC XML API Implementation Guide exists for programmatic filing, but the full enrollment/onboarding process, eligibility requirements, and any associated fees are not publicly documented. The guide mentions requiring a 'Web User Account Username' for authentication.
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to inquire about XML API access enrollment, requirements, and whether it supports bulk SEARCHING in addition to filing.

items_requiring_outreach.item: ACH Debit Account setup form and requirements
items_requiring_outreach.detail: The ACH Debit Account option for frequent filers is referenced on the site but the actual 'Authorization Agreement for Automatic Debits' form could not be downloaded (JavaScript-rendered page).
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to request the ACH Debit Account authorization form, minimum balance requirements, and setup timeline.

items_requiring_outreach.detail: The Division of Corporations (Sunbiz) offers data downloads for business entity data, but it is unclear whether bulk UCC filing data is available separately.
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Check https://dos.fl.gov/sunbiz/other-services/data-downloads/ and/or contact FloridaUCC, LLC to ask about bulk UCC data exports.

items_requiring_outreach.detail: No fax number was found for FloridaUCC, LLC during research.
items_requiring_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 to ask if fax filings are accepted and obtain the fax number if available.
